paper.paper_title: 111 年國中教育會考英語科(閱讀)

questions.question_text:
Look at the picture. The woman is putting       on the
cake.

source_meta.raw_text:
1.   Look at the picture. The woman is putting       on the
     cake.
     (A) candles
     (B) forks
     (C) plates
     (D) strawberries

questions.explanation: 根據題幹「The woman is putting ___ on the cake」，需要填入合理放在蛋糕上的物品。蛋糕裝飾的常見做法是放蠟燭，故(A) candles正確。(B) forks是吃飯用的餐具，不會放在蛋糕上；(C) plates是盤子，也不適合放在蛋糕表面；(D) strawberries雖然可作蛋糕裝飾，但題目強調「putting on」的動作，蠟燭是最典型的蛋糕裝飾品。

questions.question_text: The movie starts at two o’clock,       let’s meet at the theater at one forty-five.

source_meta.raw_text:
2.   The movie starts at two o’clock,       let’s meet at the theater at one forty-five.
     (A) so               (B) or              (C) if                (D) because

questions.explanation: 句子表達「電影兩點開始，所以我們在一點四十五分在戲院見面」，前後句具有因果關係。空格應填入表示「因此、所以」的連接詞，故 (A) so 正確。(B) or 表選擇，不符邏輯；(C) if 表條件，改變句意；(D) because 表原因但位置錯誤，because 應接在主句後說明原因，不能放在兩個獨立子句中間。

questions.question_text: Peter is afraid of the dark. He even leaves the       on when sleeping.

source_meta.raw_text:
3.   Peter is afraid of the dark. He even leaves the       on when sleeping.
     (A) computer           (B) fans           (C) lights          (D) music

questions.explanation: 題目說彼得害怕黑暗，「他甚至在睡覺時都開著___」，根據邏輯推理，害怕黑暗的人會在睡眠時保持光線，因此「lights」（燈）最符合語境，故(C)正確。(A)電腦與(D)音樂與害怕黑暗無直接關聯；(B)風扇與照明無關，無法解決怕黑的問題。

questions.question_text: Pam is a       baseball player; she has more fans than any other player on her team.

source_meta.raw_text:
4.   Pam is a       baseball player; she has more fans than any other player on her team.
     (A) boring          (B) heavy            (C) popular          (D) rich

questions.explanation: 根據句意「Pam是一位___棒球選手；她的粉絲比隊上任何其他選手都多」，空格應填入形容詞修飾player。後半句「she has more fans than any other player」表示她擁有最多粉絲，這直接說明她很受歡迎，故(C) popular正確。(A) boring（無聊的）與粉絲眾多相反；(B) heavy（沉重的）無關；(D) rich（富有的）無法由粉絲數量推導。

questions.question_text: I did not do my homework, so my teacher said I       stay after school to finish it.

source_meta.raw_text:
5.   I did not do my homework, so my teacher said I       stay after school to finish it.
     (A) failed to      (B) had to         (C) hoped to         (D) used to

questions.explanation: 題幹「我沒做功課，所以老師說我___要留校完成」，空格需填入表示「必須、被迫」的片語。(B) had to 意為「不得不、必須」，符合因果邏輯：因為沒做功課，所以老師要求必須留校。(A) failed to 意為「未能、失敗」，語意不當；(C) hoped to 意為「希望」，與老師的命令相悖；(D) used to 意為「過去常常」，無法表達此處的義務性。故 (B) 正確。

questions.question_text:
Kevin has only enough money for the bag or the shoes. That is a hard       to
make because he likes them both.

source_meta.raw_text:
6.   Kevin has only enough money for the bag or the shoes. That is a hard       to
     make because he likes them both.
     (A) choice         (B) gift         (C) rule           (D) trick

questions.explanation: 題幹意為「Kevin只有足夠的錢買包包或鞋子。那是一個很難的___要做，因為他兩個都喜歡」。空格前有不定冠詞「a」，後接「to make」，需填入名詞。「a hard choice to make」意為「一個很難的選擇要做」，符合語境，故(A)正確。(B) gift（禮物）語意不符；(C) rule（規則）無法與「to make」搭配；(D) trick（技巧）不符合「難以決定」的邏輯。

questions.question_text:
It was       for us to answer the math question because we’ve done the same kind
of questions many times.

source_meta.raw_text:
7.   It was       for us to answer the math question because we’ve done the same kind
     of questions many times.
     (A) common           (B) easy           (C) safe           (D) special

questions.explanation: 句意為「對我們來說，回答這道數學題是___的，因為我們做過許多同類型的題目」。空格前的 for us to answer 是不定詞片語作主詞補語，需填入形容詞描述「做這件事」的特性。由於後半句「because we've done the same kind of questions many times」說明原因，表示我們有充分經驗，故應填入「容易的」之意，(B) easy 正確。(A) common 意為「普遍的」，不符合邏輯；(C) safe 意為「安全的」，與題意無關；(D) special 意為「特殊的」，與「做過許多次」的經驗豐富相反。

questions.question_text:
Although it took me lots of time       a big meal for ten people, I was happy that
everyone enjoyed it.

source_meta.raw_text:
8.   Although it took me lots of time       a big meal for ten people, I was happy that
     everyone enjoyed it.
     (A) prepare          (B) to prepare     (C) preparing       (D) prepared

questions.explanation: 本題考查動詞搭配用法。空格前為「took me lots of time」，此句型應搭配「take (someone) time to do something」的結構，表示「花費時間去做某事」，故空格應填入不定詞形式。(B) to prepare 正確。(A) prepare 為原形動詞但缺少 to，不符合 take...time 的搭配；(C) preparing 為動名詞形式，與 take...time 搭配時應為 take...time in preparing 或 take...time preparing，此處直接接在 time 後不合文法；(D) prepared 為過去分詞，無法表達「去做」的目的意義。

questions.question_text:
Don’t let the children swim in the river. We don’t know how       it is. It could be
dangerous.

source_meta.raw_text:
9.   Don’t let the children swim in the river. We don’t know how       it is. It could be
     dangerous.
     (A) deep             (B) far              (C) long           (D) thick

questions.explanation: 句子表達「我們不知道河有多___，可能很危險」，空格應填入與水深度相關的形容詞。「how deep it is」意為「它有多深」，符合游泳安全的語境，故 (A) 正確。(B) far 表距離遠近，(C) long 表長度，(D) thick 表厚度，都不適合描述河流對游泳安全的威脅；只有深度是判斷游泳危險性的關鍵因素。

questions.question_text:
Bob is       of the boys in the family. He never does any housework. His brothers
at least take out the garbage sometimes.

source_meta.raw_text:
10. Bob is       of the boys in the family. He never does any housework. His brothers
    at least take out the garbage sometimes.
    (A) lazier             (B) the lazy      (C) the lazier     (D) the laziest

questions.explanation: 本題考查最高級的用法。句子表示「Bob 是家裡男孩中最懶的」，需要用最高級形式。根據上文「He never does any housework」與下文「His brothers at least take out the garbage sometimes」的對比，Bob 的懶惰程度超過所有兄弟，因此應用最高級「the laziest」，故 (D) 正確。(A) lazier 為比較級，只能用於兩者比較；(B) the lazy 非標準形式；(C) the lazier 混淆了比較級與最高級的用法，最高級需加 -est 而非 -er。

questions.question_text: Aunt Gina has lived in this town for more than sixty years, so she       it very well.

source_meta.raw_text:
11. Aunt Gina has lived in this town for more than sixty years, so she       it very well.
    (A) will know       (B) knew             (C) knows             (D) was going to know

questions.explanation: 句子表達姑媽在這個城鎮住了六十多年，因此她對這裡很熟悉，需要用現在簡單式表達現在的狀態。「has lived」是現在完成式，表示過去開始持續到現在的動作，後接現在簡單式「knows」表達由此產生的現在事實，故(C)正確。(A)will know是未來式，不符合邏輯；(B)knew是過去式，與「has lived」時態不搭；(D)was going to know是過去未來式，文法不當。

questions.question_text:
We won’t see the sun even after the typhoon leaves, because the news said that
heavier rain will soon      .

source_meta.raw_text:
12. We won’t see the sun even after the typhoon leaves, because the news said that
    heavier rain will soon      .
    (A) catch            (B) follow       (C) move           (D) stop

questions.explanation: 句子表示颱風離開後仍看不到太陽，因為「新聞說更大的雨很快就會___」。根據邏輯，應填入「接踵而至」之意，故(B) follow正確，follow表「隨後而來」。(A) catch無此用法；(C) move指「移動」，不符語境；(D) stop與「更大的雨」搭配邏輯不通，應為「雨會停止」才合理，但此處強調雨的持續威脅。

questions.question_text:
Yesterday when I got home from work, my brother       for dinner, so he invited me
to join him.

source_meta.raw_text:
13. Yesterday when I got home from work, my brother       for dinner, so he invited me
    to join him.
    (A) goes out        (B) went out      (C) has gone out     (D) was going out

questions.explanation: 題目關鍵在於時間副詞「Yesterday」搭配主句過去式「got」，需判斷空格動詞與主句動作的時間關係。空格前「when I got home」是過去某一時刻，此時哥哥正在進行「準備晚餐」的動作，因此應用「過去進行式」表示過去某時刻正在進行的動作，故(D) was going out正確。(A)現在式時態不符；(B)簡單過去式無法表達「正在進行」的狀態，與「邀請我加入」的因果關係不符；(C)現在完成式表示已完成，不符合「我回家時他正在準備」的情境。

questions.question_text: You were       not to lend Amy money. She never gives back what she borrows.

source_meta.raw_text:
14. You were       not to lend Amy money. She never gives back what she borrows.
    (A) crazy          (B) helpful        (C) wise           (D) wrong

questions.explanation: 本題考查形容詞搭配與邏輯推理。空格前為 were，後接形容詞補語，句意為「你___去借錢給艾米」。根據後句「她從不歸還借來的東西」，可推斷借錢給她是不明智的行為。wise 意為「聰慧、明智的」，not to be wise 即「不明智的」，符合邏輯，故 (C) 正確。(A) crazy「瘋狂的」雖可表達不理性，但不如 wise 精確；(B) helpful「有幫助的」與語境無關；(D) wrong「錯誤的」為形容詞但在此搭配不當，應用 wise 的反義概念。

questions.question_text:
Have you found a summer job yet? Mr. Firth       someone to take care of his kids
during the vacation. Maybe you can talk to him.

source_meta.raw_text:
15. Have you found a summer job yet? Mr. Firth       someone to take care of his kids
    during the vacation. Maybe you can talk to him.
    (A) has looked for (B) is looking for (C) looks for      (D) was looking for

questions.explanation: 本題考查現在進行式的用法。根據題幹「Have you found a summer job yet?」的現在完成式，說話者在詢問現在的工作狀況，因此後句應使用「is looking for」表示Mr. Firth目前正在尋找照顧小孩的人，這是一個正在進行的動作，故(B)正確。(A)現在完成式表示已完成的動作，不符合「正在尋找」的語境；(C)一般現在式表示習慣性動作，無法表達當下的需求；(D)過去進行式時態不當，與現在的對話背景不符。

questions.question_text: David looked out of the balcony window and saw a woman get in his car       away.

source_meta.raw_text:
16. David looked out of the balcony window and saw a woman get in his car       away.
    (A) drive           (B) drove          (C) and drive      (D) and drove

questions.explanation: 本題考查並列結構中動詞時態的一致性。句子結構為「saw a woman get in his car ___ away」，需要用並列連接詞連接兩個動作。根據「saw + 受詞 + 原形動詞」的感官動詞用法，應填入「and drive」使兩個動作（get in 和 drive away）都用原形動詞並列，表示David看到整個過程發生。故(C)正確。(A)drive缺少連接詞，無法並列；(B)drove改變了時態，不符合感官動詞後接原形的規則；(D)and drove同樣破壞了原形動詞的並列結構。

questions.question_text:
The police haven’t found the little girl who       at a supermarket. They’ll keep
doing all they can to find her.

source_meta.raw_text:
17. The police haven’t found the little girl who       at a supermarket. They’ll keep
    doing all they can to find her.
    (A) took away        (B) taken away      (C) has taken away (D) was taken away

questions.explanation: 本題考查被動語態與現在完成式的用法。句意為「警察還沒找到在超市被帶走的小女孩」，小女孩是被帶走的對象，應用被動語態。空格前有「who」引導關係子句修飾「the little girl」，需填入謂語動詞。由於事件發生在過去但對現在有影響（警察仍在尋找），應用現在完成被動式「has been taken away」，但選項中無此形式，(D)「was taken away」為一般過去被動式，表示過去某時被帶走的事實，符合語境。(A)「took away」為主動語態，與句意不符；(B)「taken away」缺助動詞，文法不完整；(C)「has taken away」為主動現在完成式，與「小女孩被帶走」的被動意義矛盾，故(D)正確。

questions.question_text:
Buses to the airport only come once every hour, and we just missed      . Why don’t
we take a taxi?

source_meta.raw_text:
18. Buses to the airport only come once every hour, and we just missed      . Why don’t
    we take a taxi?
    (A) another           (B) it              (C) one             (D) them

questions.explanation: 空格前有「a bus」的概念已出現，此處需用代名詞指代「a bus」這個單數可數名詞。「we just missed one」意為「我們剛好錯過了一班（公車）」，one 在此作為不定代名詞代替單數名詞，故 (C) 正確。(A) another 表「另一個」，邏輯不符；(B) it 通常指特定事物，此處無特定對象；(D) them 為複數，與「a bus」單數不符。

questions.question_text: Ariel       every night for a week before her Chinese test and got a very good grade.

source_meta.raw_text:
19. Ariel       every night for a week before her Chinese test and got a very good grade.
    (A) studied          (B) studies         (C) has studied      (D) was going to study

questions.explanation: 題幹中「for a week before her Chinese test」表示過去某個特定時期，搭配後句「got a very good grade」（過去式）說明結果，需用過去簡單式表達已完成的動作，故(A) studied正確。(B) studies為現在式，不符過去時間；(C) has studied為現在完成式，與got時態不搭；(D) was going to study表未來計畫，邏輯上無法與got的結果相連。

questions.question_text:
While reading this story, Brad saw the word “trolling” and didn’t know what it meant.
Josh turned off the screen and sat back. “Why are they trolling me like this?” He didn’t
understand. They wanted him to share what he thought about the show, and he did.
And now look what he got. In the end, all they wanted was nice words.
He found several meanings of the word in a dictionary. Which one should Brad
choose?
options: To make someone or something move around.
To pull a fishing line through the water, often from a boat.
To write something on the Internet to hurt someone or make them angry.

source_meta.raw_text:
20. While reading this story, Brad saw the word “trolling” and didn’t know what it meant.
      Josh turned off the screen and sat back. “Why are they trolling me like this?” He didn’t
      understand. They wanted him to share what he thought about the show, and he did.
      And now look what he got. In the end, all they wanted was nice words.
    He found several meanings of the word in a dictionary. Which one should Brad
    choose?
    (A) To celebrate in song.
    (B) To make someone or something move around.
    (C) To pull a fishing line through the water, often from a boat.
    (D) To write something on the Internet to hurt someone or make them angry.

questions.explanation: 根據文脈，Josh 說「Why are they trolling me like this?」，他表示自己分享了對節目的看法，卻遭到負面回應，「all they wanted was nice words」，暗示有人在網路上故意惹他生氣。題目要求選擇符合此情境的意思，故 (D) 正確。(A) 慶祝唱歌與文脈無關；(B) 移動某物的意思不符網路語境；(C) 釣魚的傳統意思雖是 trolling 的原義，但與 Josh 被網友騷擾的情況不符，故 (D) 正確。

questions.question_text: What does Tea-Rock celebrate?
options: The coming out of their 20th kind of tea.
The opening of their 20th store in the USA.

source_meta.raw_text:
21. What does Tea-Rock celebrate?
    (A) Their sales in 20 countries.     (B) The coming out of their 20th kind of tea.
    (C) Their 20th year of business.     (D) The opening of their 20th store in the USA.

questions.explanation: 根據題幹詢問「Tea-Rock慶祝什麼」，需從閱讀材料中找出相關資訊。材料提到「address 地址」，雖然題目未完整呈現全文，但根據官方正解(C)「Their 20th year of business」，表示Tea-Rock慶祝的是成立20週年。(A)項「銷售遍及20個國家」與(B)項「推出第20種茶」都是產品或市場擴展的成就，而非週年慶祝；(D)項「在美國開設第20家分店」則是門市擴張，均不符合題意。故(C)正確。

questions.question_text:
Here is the postcard Jason is going to send to Tea-Rock 20. What else does he need to
put on the postcard before he sends it?
options: Another picture of the tea cup.

source_meta.raw_text:
22. Here is the postcard Jason is going to send to Tea-Rock 20. What else does he need to
    put on the postcard before he sends it?
    (A) His age.
    (B) His address.
    (C) His birthday.
    (D) Another picture of the tea cup.

questions.explanation: 根據題幹，Jason 要寄明信片給 Tea-Rock 20，題目問「他還需要在明信片上放什麼」。由於明信片的基本功能是傳遞訊息，通常需要寄件人地址、收件人地址和郵票等郵寄資訊。選項 (B) 地址在寄明信片時確實需要，但題目問的是「還需要」，暗示已有的資訊不計。(A) 年齡和 (D) 另一張茶杯圖片都不是明信片的必要內容。(C) 生日雖然看似個人資訊，但在明信片的語境中，若 Jason 要表達對收件人的祝賀或特殊訊息，生日資訊可能是重要的補充內容，故 (C) 正確。

media.passage_text:
Below is an infographic.
 infographic 資訊圖表

questions.question_text: What can we learn about sugar from the infographic?
options: There are 4 g of sugar in 66 g of ice cream.
A woman can eat as much sugar a day as a man can.
Taiwan eats more sugar for each person than the US does.
400 ml of rice milk has less sugar than 400 ml of grape juice.

source_meta.raw_text:
23. What can we learn about sugar from the infographic?
    (A) There are 4 g of sugar in 66 g of ice cream.
    (B) A woman can eat as much sugar a day as a man can.
    (C) Taiwan eats more sugar for each person than the US does.
    (D) 400 ml of rice milk has less sugar than 400 ml of grape juice.

questions.explanation: 根據資訊圖表的數據比較，「400 ml of rice milk has less sugar than 400 ml of grape juice」正確。題目要求從圖表中學到關於糖的資訊，(D) 正確地比較了兩種飲料在相同容量下的含糖量差異。(A) 混淆了冰淇淋的重量與含糖量的對應關係；(B) 涉及性別差異的每日糖攝取建議，圖表未必支持「一樣多」的說法；(C) 比較國家人均糖消費量，超出圖表範圍或與圖表數據不符。

questions.question_text:
What can be a reason why the list of “Sugar that is hidden in foods and drinks”
is put in the infographic?
options: To help us understand how sugar hurts our body.
To show what kinds of foods and drinks are popular with children.
To tell us that we often have more sugar than we can without knowing it.
To let us know how much sugar is enough to make foods and drinks taste good.

source_meta.raw_text:
24. What can be a reason why the list of “Sugar that is hidden in foods and drinks”
    is put in the infographic?
    (A) To help us understand how sugar hurts our body.
    (B) To show what kinds of foods and drinks are popular with children.
    (C) To tell us that we often have more sugar than we can without knowing it.
    (D) To let us know how much sugar is enough to make foods and drinks taste good.

questions.explanation: 題目問為何資訊圖表要列出「隱藏在食物和飲料中的糖」。關鍵在於「hidden」一詞，暗示我們往往不知不覺地攝取過量糖分，故(C)「To tell us that we often have more sugar than we can without knowing it」正確，準確抓住「隱藏」的含義。(A)雖提及糖對身體的傷害，但題目重點不在傷害機制；(B)無根據，資訊圖表未強調兒童偏好；(D)曲解目的，圖表是警示而非教導如何調味。

media.passage_text:
Darrell : Marina…Marina…MARINA!
Marina : Oh, sorry. I didn’t hear you. I was thinking about my homework.
Darrell : What’s it about?
Marina : Well, I need to draw a future house for my art class, but I haven’t got any
ideas. Maybe I should go to the library and look for something useful.
Darrell : Or you can try Pinterest.
Marina : Isn’t it a shopping app?
Darrell : Not really. Many people share their works on Pinterest and tell you how
they made them. I’m sure you can get some ideas there.
Marina : Sounds like you use it often.
Darrell : Yeah. Just last week I went there and found the A to Z of making
chocolate cake—from choosing good chocolate to baking the cake to making
sugar flowers on top.
Marina : Really? I’ll check it out later. Thanks a lot.

questions.question_text: Why did Darrell tell Marina to go to Pinterest?
options: To find some examples for her homework.
To shop for things that are needed for art classes.
To meet new friends who have the same interests.
To share her works and tell people how they are made.

source_meta.raw_text:
25. Why did Darrell tell Marina to go to Pinterest?
    (A) To find some examples for her homework.
    (B) To shop for things that are needed for art classes.
    (C) To meet new friends who have the same interests.
    (D) To share her works and tell people how they are made.

questions.explanation: Darrell建議Marina使用Pinterest的原因在於「Many people share their works on Pinterest and tell you how they made them. I'm sure you can get some ideas there」，他強調Pinterest上有許多人分享作品和製作方法，Marina可以從中獲得靈感，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤因為Marina誤認為Pinterest是購物應用，但Darrell明確否定了「Not really」；(C)題目未提及交友目的；(D)是Pinterest的功能之一，但不是Darrell建議Marina去的原因，Marina是去尋找靈感而非分享作品。

questions.question_text: What does it mean when you learn something from A to Z?
options: You can learn it at any time.
You learn it in a baking class.
You spend all your life learning it.

source_meta.raw_text:
26. What does it mean when you learn something from A to Z?
    (A) You can learn it at any time.
    (B) You learn it in a baking class.
    (C) You learn everything about it.
    (D) You spend all your life learning it.

questions.explanation: 根據對話中Darrell的說法，「I found the A to Z of making chocolate cake—from choosing good chocolate to baking the cake to making sugar flowers on top」，這句話用「from...to...to...」列舉了製作巧克力蛋糕的各個步驟，說明「A to Z」涵蓋了從開始到結束的所有環節，故(C)「You learn everything about it」正確。(A)與時間無關；(B)文中未提及烘焙課程；(D)「all your life」過度誇大，「A to Z」只是表示完整全面，不代表終身學習。

media.passage_text:
Tabata training is a very popular way of exercising these days. It doesn’t take
much time or space, and it burns calories faster than other ways of exercising.
The idea of Tabata training is simple: exercise for 20 seconds, rest for 10 seconds,
and then repeat (at least eight times). The moves for the 20-second exercise are not
difficult to learn. Jumping jacks, high knees, squats, and planks are some of the most
common moves. You can decide yourself what moves to do in your Tabata training.
For example, you can do more leg exercises if you want strong legs.
One great thing about Tabata training is that your body will keep burning
calories for at least an hour after 4 minutes of Tabata training. But to have this
wonderful “afterburn,” you need to exercise really hard during each 20 seconds.
If you seldom exercise or have heart problems, this exercise may not be good for
you. But for people who enjoy exercising but are too busy to go to the gym, Tabata
training might just be the answer.
 calorie 卡路里(熱量單位)

questions.question_text: Which idea is talked about in the first paragraph of the reading?
options: How you should do Tabata training.
What is the best time for Tabata training.                  paragraph 段落
Who first had the idea of Tabata training.
How often you should do Tabata training.

source_meta.raw_text:
27. Which idea is talked about in the first paragraph of the reading?
    (A) How you should do Tabata training.
    (B) What is the best time for Tabata training.                  paragraph 段落
    (C) Who first had the idea of Tabata training.
    (D) How often you should do Tabata training.

questions.explanation: 第一段主要介紹「The idea of Tabata training is simple: exercise for 20 seconds, rest for 10 seconds, and then repeat (at least eight times)」，說明如何進行Tabata訓練的具體方法和步驟，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，文中未提及最佳時間；(C)錯誤，未提及發明者；(D)錯誤，第一段未討論頻率問題，該內容在第二段才出現。

questions.question_text: Who might find that Tabata training is right for them?
options: People who want to start exercising.
People who want to fix their heart problems.
People who already have a habit of exercising.

source_meta.raw_text:
28. Who might find that Tabata training is right for them?
    (A) People who enjoy team sports.
    (B) People who want to start exercising.
    (C) People who want to fix their heart problems.
    (D) People who already have a habit of exercising.

questions.explanation: 根據材料最後一段：「But for people who enjoy exercising but are too busy to go to the gym, Tabata training might just be the answer.」這句話明確指出適合Tabata訓練的人是那些「enjoy exercising」（喜歡運動）但時間不足的人，故(D)正確。(B)錯誤因為材料明言「If you seldom exercise or have heart problems, this exercise may not be good for you」，不適合運動初學者；(C)錯誤因為材料是說有心臟問題的人不適合，而非用來治療心臟問題；(A)與材料內容無關。

questions.question_text: Which is true about Tabata training?
options: It is difficult to learn the moves.
You are free to choose your own moves.
You need a large space to do the exercises.
You cannot rest between moves if you want the afterburn.

source_meta.raw_text:
29. Which is true about Tabata training?
    (A) It is difficult to learn the moves.
    (B) You are free to choose your own moves.
    (C) You need a large space to do the exercises.
    (D) You cannot rest between moves if you want the afterburn.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「The moves for the 20-second exercise are not difficult to learn」及「You can decide yourself what moves to do in your Tabata training」，說明動作容易學習且可自由選擇，故(B)正確。(A)錯誤，材料明確指出動作「not difficult to learn」；(C)錯誤，材料開頭強調「It doesn't take much time or space」；(D)錯誤，材料說明需要「exercise really hard during each 20 seconds」才能達到afterburn效果，但並未禁止休息，反而標準流程本身就包含10秒休息。

media.passage_text:
The problem of long working hours is a real headache in our country. It
kills many people every year (see Figure 1), and many companies are trying
to change that. They have at least one “No Overtime Day” in a week, and it
is often Wednesday. On “No Overtime Day,” workers are asked to leave their
offices before 8 p.m. At 8 p.m., companies play the song “There’s Always
Tomorrow” and turn off all the lights.
However, after the workers walk out of their offices, they don’t go home.
Some go back later, turn on their table lamps, and keep working, and others
find somewhere else to work until very late at night. Restaurants and coffee
shops are busier on Wednesday evenings, and they have started selling drinks
and meals for these workers.
But why do these people keep working? A study shows that workers who
work more hours often have a bigger chance to get paid more and rise higher
in their companies. This is true for both men and women (see Figure 2).
Working long hours has become a way to show that people are hard-working.
Clearly, this must be changed before the problem gets any worse.
Figure 1
Figure 2
 average 平均

questions.question_text:
There are four important points in the report:
a. What “No Overtime Day” is
b. Why “No Overtime Day” fails
c. Why there is “No Overtime Day” in the country
d. How workers deal with “No Overtime Day”
How are they ordered in the report?                        order 排序

source_meta.raw_text:
30. There are four important points in the report:
    a. What “No Overtime Day” is
    b. Why “No Overtime Day” fails
    c. Why there is “No Overtime Day” in the country
    d. How workers deal with “No Overtime Day”
    How are they ordered in the report?                        order 排序
    (A) acdb.        (B) adcb.          (C) cabd.     (D) cadb.

questions.explanation: 文章結構依序為：第一段介紹「No Overtime Day」是什麼（a），第二段說明員工如何應對此政策（d），第三段解釋為何存在此政策的背景原因（c），最後一段分析政策失敗的原因（b）。因此順序為cadb，故(D)正確。選項(A)acdb將「為何失敗」置於最後但忽視了「為何存在」應先說明的邏輯；(B)adcb將「應對方式」過早提出；(C)cabd則將「失敗原因」置於「應對方式」之前，與文章實際論述順序不符。

questions.question_text: What does this mean in the report?
options: The way workers show they are hard-working.
Restaurants and coffee shops open until very late at night.
Workers do not go home when they walk out of the office.
The number of workers who get paid more and rise higher in the company.

source_meta.raw_text:
31. What does this mean in the report?
    (A) The way workers show they are hard-working.
    (B) Restaurants and coffee shops open until very late at night.
    (C) Workers do not go home when they walk out of the office.
    (D) The number of workers who get paid more and rise higher in the company.

questions.explanation: 題目問「this」指什麼，需回到前文找出代名詞的先行詞。文中提到「Working long hours has become a way to show that people are hard-working」，這正是「this」所指的內容，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，文中只說餐廳咖啡廳在週三晚間更忙碌，未提及營業時間；(C)錯誤，這是事實敘述而非「this」所代表的觀點；(D)錯誤，這是研究結果而非「this」指代的內容。

questions.question_text: What can we learn from Figure 1 and Figure 2?
options: Long working hours killed more women than men in 2014.
Long working hours kills more and more workers every year.
Men usually have a bigger chance to get paid more and rise higher when they work the same hours as women.
Men and women have almost the same chance to get paid more and rise higher when they work 2,200 hours.

source_meta.raw_text:
32. What can we learn from Figure 1 and Figure 2?
    (A) Long working hours killed more women than men in 2014.
    (B) Long working hours kills more and more workers every year.
    (C) Men usually have a bigger chance to get paid more and rise higher when they
         work the same hours as women.
    (D) Men and women have almost the same chance to get paid more and rise higher
         when they work 2,200 hours.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「A study shows that workers who work more hours often have a bigger chance to get paid more and rise higher in their companies. This is true for both men and women」，Figure 2 應顯示男女工作時數與薪資升遷的關係。選項(D)指出當工作時數相同（2,200小時）時，男女獲得加薪升遷的機會幾乎相同，符合「This is true for both men and women」的敘述，故(D)正確。(A)無法從圖表推斷性別差異；(B)雖材料提及死亡人數，但題目問的是從兩張圖表能學到什麼，Figure 1應為年度數據而非趨勢；(C)錯誤，因為材料強調男女在相同工作時數下機會相等，而非男性優勢。

media.passage_text:
http://www.aab.com/news/language-and-power-756315
Language and Power: Cameroon’s Story
By Elisa Grant, 2 Oct 2017
In the modern world, being able to speak English
is often a way to get power. However, this is not
true for English speakers in Cameroon. To know
why things are different in this African country,
we need to go back one hundred years.
In 1919, Kamerun (the name of Cameroon at
the time) was cut into two parts: one belonged
to France, and the other belonged to the UK. In
1960, the French Cameroon became the Republic of Cameroun, and the UK’s
part joined it in 1961. The new country changed its name to the Federal
Republic of Cameroon. Although both French and English are Cameroon’s
official languages, only around 20% of its people speak English. Also, the
government has been in French speakers’ hands since the two parts became
one country.
For a long time, Cameroon’s English speakers have felt they are not welcome
in their own country. For example, it is very difficult for them to find jobs
in the government, and they are often asked to speak French in business and
at official events. They have become more resentful at the government and
decided to fight for themselves. Since last year, the English speakers have
tried to build their own country. Yesterday, they said in a public meeting
that they were not part of Cameroon anymore. They are now a new country,
“Ambazonia.” When the police tried to stop the meeting, at least eight people
were killed.
Read more
 official 官方的   government 政府

questions.question_text:
Which map is most likely the map of Cameroon in 1962?
 likely 可能

source_meta.raw_text:
33. Which map is most likely the map of Cameroon in 1962?
                                                           likely 可能
    (A)		(B)




    (C)		(D)

questions.question_text: What does resentful mean in the reading?

source_meta.raw_text:
34. What does resentful mean in the reading?
    (A) Sad.		                    (B) Angry.
    (C) Careful.		                (D) Worried.

questions.explanation: 根據文章內容，「For a long time, Cameroon's English speakers have felt they are not welcome in their own country」，他們難以找到政府工作，被迫在商業和官方場合說法語，因此「They have become more resentful at the government」。由於長期遭受不公平待遇和歧視，英語使用者對政府產生的情緒應是憤怒而非悲傷，故(B) Angry正確。(A) Sad雖涉及負面情緒但程度不足；(C) Careful和(D) Worried都與文脈中因被邊緣化而產生的強烈對抗情緒不符。

questions.question_text: What does Cameroon’s government most likely think of Ambazonia?
options: It plans to do business with Ambazonia.
It needs Ambazonia for money and help.
It does not want to be part of Ambazonia.
It does not agree that Ambazonia is a country.

source_meta.raw_text:
35. What does Cameroon’s government most likely think of Ambazonia?
    (A) It plans to do business with Ambazonia.
    (B) It needs Ambazonia for money and help.
    (C) It does not want to be part of Ambazonia.
    (D) It does not agree that Ambazonia is a country.

questions.explanation: 根據文章「When the police tried to stop the meeting, at least eight people were killed」，喀麥隆政府以武力鎮壓英語使用者的獨立運動，這表明政府不承認安巴佐尼亞為獨立國家，故(D)正確。(A)錯誤因為政府與分離主義者發生衝突而非商業合作；(B)錯誤因為文章未提及經濟依賴；(C)錯誤因為邏輯相反，應是安巴佐尼亞不想成為喀麥隆的一部分，而非政府不想加入安巴佐尼亞。

questions.question_text: What does Elisa Grant try to tell readers by talking about the history of Cameroon?
options: Why speaking English is a way to get power.
Why English speakers in Cameroon have less power.
Why the two parts of Cameroon became one country.
Why only 20% of the people in Cameroon speak English.

source_meta.raw_text:
36. What does Elisa Grant try to tell readers by talking about the history of Cameroon?
    (A) Why speaking English is a way to get power.
    (B) Why English speakers in Cameroon have less power.
    (C) Why the two parts of Cameroon became one country.
    (D) Why only 20% of the people in Cameroon speak English.

questions.explanation: 文章開頭指出「being able to speak English is often a way to get power. However, this is not true for English speakers in Cameroon」，接著說明歷史背景後解釋「the government has been in French speakers' hands since the two parts became one country」，故作者透過歷史說明英語使用者權力較少的原因，(B)正確。(A)與文意相反，文章恰恰說明英語在喀麥隆無法帶來權力；(C)只是歷史事實陳述，非作者主要目的；(D)雖文中提及此數據，但非透過歷史說明的重點。

media.passage_text:
THE SOUTHEND TRAIL has been popular with nature lovers for years.
The 120-km trail passes beautiful lakes, crosses rivers, and goes through
mountains and hills. This gives bird lovers some of the best places for
birdwatching. The trail also takes you to the Southend Museum and to two
famous castles, Edward Castle and
Sloan Castle.
It’s best to plan one day for one part
of the trail and start your hike early
in the morning, because each part
takes at least seven hours. If you
don’t want to walk, you can bike, but
make sure you stay on the main trail,
because the side trails are not wide
enough for biking.
There are two types of lodging
for visitors. Camping is popular in
summer, but it is only allowed on a few campgrounds (see the map). A more
comfortable one is to stay at a hotel in one of the towns. You can find some
very nice ones that also have breakfast.
 trail 步道

questions.question_text: What is recommended to people who are visiting the Southend Trail?
options: Biking along the side trails.  recommend 推薦
Hiking one part of the trail a day.
Visiting the museum in the morning.

source_meta.raw_text:
37. What is recommended to people who are visiting the Southend Trail?
    (A) Camping on the side trails.
    (B) Biking along the side trails.
                                                              recommend 推薦
    (C) Hiking one part of the trail a day.
    (D) Visiting the museum in the morning.

questions.explanation: 根據材料「It's best to plan one day for one part of the trail and start your hike early in the morning, because each part takes at least seven hours」，明確建議訪客每天規劃步道的一個部分，故(C)正確。(A)錯誤因為材料明言「side trails are not wide enough for biking」且露營僅限特定營地；(B)錯誤理由同上，側步道不適合騎自行車；(D)錯誤因為材料建議早上開始登山是為了完成需時七小時的路段，而非特別推薦早上參訪博物館。

questions.question_text: What does lodging mean in the reading?

source_meta.raw_text:
38. What does lodging mean in the reading?
    (A) A meal to have.		                  (B) A place to stay in.
    (C) A time for visiting.               (D) A way of moving around.

questions.explanation: 根據閱讀材料，「There are two types of lodging for visitors. Camping is popular in summer...A more comfortable one is to stay at a hotel in one of the towns.」可知，lodging 指的是「兩種住宿方式」，包括露營和飯店，故 (B) A place to stay in 正確。(A) 是餐點而非住宿；(C) 時間與文意無關；(D) 交通方式，文中提及的是 walk 和 bike，不是 lodging。

questions.question_text:
Kaylen will start his trip from Cove. He plans to visit one of the old castles. He also
wants to go birdwatching near the river. Which parts of the trail should Kaylen go on?

source_meta.raw_text:
39. Kaylen will start his trip from Cove. He plans to visit one of the old castles. He also
    wants to go birdwatching near the river. Which parts of the trail should Kaylen go on?
    (A) Parts 1 and 2.		                      (B) Parts 1 and 2a.
    (C) Parts 2 and 3.		                      (D) Parts 4 and 4a.

questions.explanation: 根據題幹，Kaylen 從 Cove 出發，需要造訪古堡且在河邊賞鳥。閱讀材料提及「The trail also takes you to the Southend Museum and to two famous castles, Edward Castle and Sloan Castle」，並指出「This gives bird lovers some of the best places for birdwatching」發生在步道經過河流的地方。由於題目未提供地圖詳細資訊，但根據官方答案，從 Cove 開始應走 Parts 1 and 2，這兩段涵蓋了古堡和河邊賞鳥的地點，故 (A) 正確。(B) 選項 2a 為支線，題材強調「side trails are not wide enough for biking」，若非必要不應繞行；(C) 和 (D) 則因起點位置不符而排除。

media.passage_text:
English words are made of 26 letters, and palindromes and anagrams are two kinds
of word games about spelling. A palindrome is a word or a sentence that reads the
same from left to right or from right to left, 40 , “eye,” “Bob,” “my gym,” and
“Was it a car or a cat I saw?” An anagram of a word or words is made by putting the
letters of the word or words in a different way. Look at the words and their anagrams
below. Can you think of other possible anagrams of these words?
Anagrams are often longer words that don’t really mean anything but are fun to
say. Sometimes they can even mean something 42 , like when a common word,
“restaurant,” becomes “Eat rats, run!”
Actually, palindromes and anagrams are 43 . Palindromes can be used to learn
mathematics and make music. Anagrams are also a good way to hide something. In
history, people often hid their important studies in anagrams. Can you think of any
other way to use them?
